name: Mendix
what_it_does: Low-code application development platform with a REST API for app lifecycle management, OData-based data access, deployment automation, and user management.
use_cases: Triggering Mendix app deployments from an orchestration agent or CI/CD pipeline
Querying and mutating Mendix app data through auto-generated OData endpoints
Managing Mendix environments, packages, and user roles programmatically
Monitoring app performance and deployment status via Platform API
Automating Mendix project scaffolding and configuration using the Mendix SDK
not_for: Teams not already using the Mendix platform — the API only manages Mendix apps
High-frequency transactional data workloads better served by purpose-built databases
best_when: Best when you need to automate lifecycle operations (deploy, manage, query) for applications already built on the Mendix low-code platform.
avoid_when: Avoid when your goal is general-purpose REST API development — this API manages the Mendix platform itself, not arbitrary application logic.

auth.notes: Platform API uses personal access tokens. App-level OData endpoints support basic auth and OAuth2. Scope-based permissions per API surface.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free Community plan: 1 app, limited environments, no SLA
pricing.paid_tiers: Basic from $50/user/month; Standard and Enterprise from $400/user/month with full API access
pricing.notes: Full Platform API access requires paid plans. Community plan is limited to experimentation.

performance.rate_limits: API rate limits are documented per endpoint; varies by plan tier

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Platform API returns structured JSON errors. OData errors follow OData spec formatting. Documentation is thorough for common flows.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Deployment triggers are not idempotent. OData read operations are idempotent; write operations vary.
known_agent_gotchas: OData endpoint URL structure is app-specific and must be discovered per environment
SDK (Model SDK) is for model manipulation at design time, not runtime data access
Deployment pipeline has asynchronous steps; agents must poll for completion status

security_score_components.security_notes: SOC2 Type II certified. OAuth2 with scoped access tokens. Enterprise version supports SSO and role-based access control.
